examples.title: Quality Gate Review Process
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating a typical quality gate review process in software development
examples.code:
PROCEDURE QualityGateReview(phase, deliverables)
 BEGIN
 checklist ← GetPhaseChecklist(phase)
 score ← 0
 FOR each criterion IN checklist DO
 IF EvaluateCriterion(deliverables, criterion) THEN
 score ← score + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 passPercentage ← (score / checklist.length) * 100
 
 IF passPercentage >= MIN_QUALITY_THRESHOLD THEN
 RETURN APPROVED
 ELSE
 RETURN REJECTED_WITH_ISSUES
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the concept of quality gates - checkpoints at each project phase that must be passed before proceeding. It evaluates deliverables against defined criteria and requires a minimum quality threshold to pass.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of checklist criteria

examples.title: Defect Density Calculation
examples.description: Calculate defect density metric for quality measurement
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CalculateDefectDensity(totalDefects, linesOfCode)
 BEGIN
 IF linesOfCode = 0 THEN
 RETURN 0 // Avoid division by zero
 END IF
 
 // Defect density = Defects per thousand lines of code
 defectDensity ← (totalDefects / linesOfCode) * 1000
 
 // Categorize based on industry benchmarks
 IF defectDensity < 1.0 THEN
 rating ← "EXCELLENT"
 ELSE IF defectDensity < 5.0 THEN
 rating ← "GOOD"
 ELSE IF defectDensity < 10.0 THEN
 rating << "ACCEPTABLE"
 ELSE
 rating ← "POOR - Requires improvement"
 END IF
 
 RETURN {density: defectDensity, rating: rating}
 END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Defect density is a key software quality metric. Industry benchmarks: <1 defect/KLOC = excellent, 1-5 = good, 5-10 = acceptable, >10 = poor quality. This metric helps compare module quality and estimate testing efforts.

examples.title: Quality Control Test Execution
examples.description: Simulate test execution with quality pass/fail decision
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ExecuteTestSuite(testCases)
 BEGIN
 totalTests ← testCases.length
 passed ← 0
 failed ← 0
 criticalDefects ← []
 
 FOR each testCase IN testCases DO
 result ← RunTest(testCase)
 
 IF result.status = PASS THEN
 passed ← passed + 1
 ELSE
 failed ← failed + 1
 IF result.severity = CRITICAL THEN
 criticalDefects.Add(result)
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 qualityMetrics ← {
 totalTests: totalTests,
 passed: passed,
 failed: failed,
 passRate: (passed / totalTests) * 100,
 criticalDefects: criticalDefects
 }
 
 // Decision: Release only if no critical defects and pass rate >= 95%
 IF criticalDefects.length = 0 AND qualityMetrics.passRate >= 95 THEN
 RETURN {decision: "RELEASE", metrics: qualityMetrics}
 ELSE
 RETURN {decision: "DO NOT RELEASE", metrics: qualityMetrics}
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This demonstrates Quality Control in action - executing tests, calculating metrics (pass rate), and making go/no-go release decisions based on quality thresholds. Critical defects automatically block release regardless of overall pass rate.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of test cases
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k is the number of critical defects
